Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications Chapter Map

Full reading map for Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications chapter notes.

Source

This map uses selected MCP research-library sections from Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications (Stufflebeam and Coryn 2014).

Reading Route

Part One: Fundamentals of Evaluation

  • Chapter 1: Overview of the Evaluation Field - pages 39-79; Part One: Fundamentals of Evaluation > Chapter 1: Overview of the Evaluation Field
  • Chapter 2: Evaluation Theory - pages 81-102; Part One: Fundamentals of Evaluation > Chapter 2: Evaluation Theory
  • Chapter 3: Standards for Program Evaluations - pages 105-138; Part One: Fundamentals of Evaluation > Chapter 3: Standards for Program Evaluations

Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models

  • Chapter 4: Background for Assessing Evaluation Approaches - pages 143-150; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 4: Background for Assessing Evaluation Approaches
  • Chapter 5: Pseudoevaluations - pages 151-168; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 5: Pseudoevaluations
  • Chapter 6: Quasi-evaluation Studies - pages 169-208; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 6: Quasi-evaluation Studies
  • Chapter 7: Improvement- and Accountability-oriented Evaluation Approaches - pages 209-226; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 7: Improvement- and Accountability-oriented Evaluation Approaches
  • Chapter 8: Social Agenda and Advocacy Evaluation Approaches - pages 227-245; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 8: Social Agenda and Advocacy Evaluation Approaches
  • Chapter 9: Eclectic Evaluation Approaches - pages 247-261; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 9: Eclectic Evaluation Approaches
  • Chapter 10: Best Approaches for Twenty-first-century Evaluations - pages 265-281; Part Two: An Evaluation of Evaluation Approaches and Models > Chapter 10: Best Approaches for Twenty-first-century Evaluations

Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches

  • Chapter 11: Experimental and Quasi-experimental Design Evaluations - pages 285-324; Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches > Chapter 11: Experimental and Quasi-experimental Design Evaluations
  • Chapter 12: Case Study Evaluations - pages 327-342; Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches > Chapter 12: Case Study Evaluations
  • Chapter 13: Daniel Stufflebeam’s CIPP Model for Evaluation - pages 345-375; Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches > Chapter 13: Daniel Stufflebeam's CIPP Model for Evaluation: an Improvement- and Accountability-oriented Approach
  • Chapter 14: Michael Scriven’s Consumer-oriented Approach to Evaluation - pages 377-405; Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches > Chapter 14: Michael Scriven's Consumer-oriented Approach to Evaluation
  • Chapter 15: Robert Stake’s Responsive or Stakeholder-centered Evaluation Approach - pages 409-435; Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches > Chapter 15: Robert Stake's Responsive or Stakeholder-centered Evaluation Approach
  • Chapter 16: Michael Patton’s Utilization-focused Evaluation - pages 439-453; Part Three: Explication of Selected Evaluation Approaches > Chapter 16: Michael Patton's Utilization-focused Evaluation

Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools

  • Chapter 17: Identifying and Assessing Evaluation Opportunities - pages 459-468; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 17: Identifying and Assessing Evaluation Opportunities
  • Chapter 18: First Steps in Addressing Evaluation Opportunities - pages 471-479; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 18: First Steps in Addressing Evaluation Opportunities
  • Chapter 19: Designing Evaluations - pages 481-510; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 19: Designing Evaluations
  • Chapter 20: Budgeting Evaluations - pages 515-539; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 20: Budgeting Evaluations
  • Chapter 21: Contracting Evaluations - pages 541-554; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 21: Contracting Evaluations
  • Chapter 22: Collecting Evaluative Information - pages 555-579; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 22: Collecting Evaluative Information
  • Chapter 23: Analyzing and Synthesizing Information - pages 593-624; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 23: Analyzing and Synthesizing Information
  • Chapter 24: Communicating Evaluation Findings - pages 625-662; Part Four: Evaluation Tasks, Procedures, and Tools > Chapter 24: Communicating Evaluation Findings

Part Five: Metaevaluation and Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation

  • Chapter 25: Metaevaluation: Evaluating Evaluations - pages 667-705; Part Five: Metaevaluation and Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation > Chapter 25: Metaevaluation: Evaluating Evaluations
  • Chapter 26: Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation - pages 707-725; Part Five: Metaevaluation and Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation > Chapter 26: Institutionalizing and Mainstreaming Evaluation

How to Use This Map

  • Start with Part One to ground definitions, standards, and theory.
  • Use Part Two to compare evaluation approaches and understand the authors’ standards-based judgments.
  • Use Part Three when choosing among selected models such as CIPP, consumer-oriented, responsive, or utilization-focused evaluation.
  • Use Part Four as a procedural guide for scoping, designing, budgeting, contracting, collecting, analyzing, reporting, and follow-up.
  • Use Part Five to check evaluation quality and organizational evaluation capacity.

Concept Map

Seed Notes

  • Program Evaluation Standards and Theory Development
  • CIPP Model Overview
  • Standards and Metaevaluation
